question: What's the best time to visit Mission Beach QLD with kids?
answer: May to October is absolutely brilliant for families! You'll get gorgeous weather without the stingers, so the kids can swim safely without stinger suits. School holidays (June-July and September) get busy, so book ahead. April and November are risky for stingers but less crowded. Avoid January-March – it's cyclone season and stinger territory. The dry season means calm seas, perfect beach days, and ideal conditions for spotting cassowaries with the little ones!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Mission Beach QLD?
answer: Yes mate, several Mission Beach holiday parks welcome your furry family members! Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be leashed and well-behaved. You'll typically need to bring your pet's bedding and keep them off furniture. Some parks have designated pet-friendly cabins or sites. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy, any size restrictions, and additional fees. The nearby beaches have off-leash areas where your pooch can have a ripper time!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Mission Beach QLD during school holidays?
answer: Expect to pay $150-$300 per night for cabins during school holidays, while powered sites run $45-$80. Prices peak in June-July and September holidays. Easter's also pricey. Book 3-6 months ahead for better rates and availability. Off-peak (Feb-May, Oct-Nov) you'll save 30-40%. Most parks offer 'stay 7, pay 6' deals outside peak times. Budget-friendly tip: powered sites with your own caravan save heaps compared to cabins!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Definitely bring stinger suits (May-October you're usually right, but better safe!), reef-safe sunscreen, insect repellent, and a basic first aid kit. Pack beach toys, bikes or scooters, and outdoor games. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO favourite snacks. Don't forget a portable fan, night lights, and familiar bedding for better sleep. Wet weather gear's handy year-round. Download some movies beforehand – park WiFi can be dodgy!

question: What is there to do near Mission Beach QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of fun! Mission Beach has 14km of gorgeous beaches perfect for sandcastle building. Visit the cassowary conservation centre, take a cruise to Dunk Island, or try gentle kayaking through mangroves. The jungle walking tracks are magic – kids love spotting wildlife. There's a water sports hub for paddle boarding, mini golf, playgrounds, and the Paronella Park castle's nearby. Kurrimine Beach (20min south) has an awesome natural rock pool for littlies!
